records.ety: [Cf. OE. larege (Cotgrave), It. larice, Sp. larice, alerce, G. lärche; all fr. L. larix, -icis, Gr. la`rix.]

defs: A genus of coniferous trees, having deciduous leaves, in fascicles (see Illust. of Fascicle).
notes: The European larch is Larix Europæa. The American or black larch is Larix Americana, the hackmatack or tamarack. The trees are generally of a drooping, graceful appearance.
